Lorea & Amy's Recipes
  • List

Garlic Mashed Potatoes

Want to hear the creator's life story? Click here.
Words of Wisdom
Honestly, this recipe is the first I've encountered in a while where I have no adjustmets or advice. These are absolutely the best mashed potatoes I've ever had and it's a simple, straightforward recipe!
Yield 10 servings
Preparation time 15m
Cook time 15m
Total time 30m
Ingredients
  • 4 lb Yukon gold potatoes (approx. 10)
  • 1 1/3 Cup whole milk
  • 6 cloves garlic, smashed
  • 8 Tbsp unsalted butter, softened
  • 1/4 Cup sour cream
  • 1 1/2 tsp fine sea salt
  • (optional) 2 Tbsp chives, finely chopped for garnish
Instructions
  1. Peel potatoes and cut into approximately same sized chunks (typically halved or quartered, depending on potato size). While preparing, place potato chunks in a bowl of cold water to reduce startch and preven discoloration.
  2. Place potato chunks into a pot and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer.
  3. Cook for 15-18 minutes or until fork tender but not falling apart.
  4. When potatoes are almost finished, heat milk, butter, and smashed garlic cloves in a small sauce pan over medium heat until it begins steaming. Remove from heat and strain using small sieve to remove garlic cloves, which can be discarded. Set mixture aside.
  5. Drain potatoes and place pot back over very low heat.
  6. Mash potatoes immediately using hand masher or electric mixer. Drizzle in milk mixture until potatoes reach desired texture.
  7. Add sour cream and salt, to taste. Garnish with chives if desired.
